creator: _jkrow
caption:
“You have your whole life to travel”
Is, in my opinion, one of the biggest lies American society teaches us.

I still remember being 23, surrounded by people who taught me to go to school, settle down, work hard, save my money, retire. And they finished it with, “and THEN you can travel. You have your whole life left.”

But personally, I’ve never subscribed to that. I had 10 vacation days and a sense of urgency.

So I booked the cheapest flight I could find to Hawaii. 

After that, I started taking road trips on weekends, flying out on Friday nights, squeezing everyyyy single ounce of PTO to take bucket list trips and using my evenings after work to chase sunsets.

Because the longer you wait, the harder it gets. That’s a fact. 

In my 30s, I’ve learned that life doesn’t open up to you. You have to take it. And if someone tells you to wait until later because you have your entire life left, tell them that later isn’t promised. 

✈️ What was the trip that made you stop waiting for later?

👉 And if you don’t have much time to spare, say “weekend” and I’ll send you my weekend getaways guide for some fun ideas of where you can spend your next 3 days of PTO.

transcription.text: And he's talking with David, who's still in the Navy, and probably will be alright.
